query: Why are API requests returning 503 after deploy?

concepts.title: Debug 503 After Deploy
concepts.why: Provides the diagnostic sequence for elevated API Gateway 503 responses after a deployment.
concepts.summary: Check rate-limit changes, gateway logs, auth middleware behavior, route-level error rates, and request-routing tests before rollback or hotfix.
caveats: Do not change database schema while debugging gateway 503s.
Do not disable auth middleware to reduce errors.
Do not assume the database is the cause unless gateway logs show database timeouts.

concepts.title: API Gateway
concepts.why: Defines the service responsible for auth, rate limits, routing, metrics, and logs.
concepts.summary: API Gateway receives customer API traffic, applies authentication and rate limits, then routes requests to internal services.
caveats: Do not bypass auth middleware for customer routes.
Rate-limit changes must be tested with Request Routing Tests.
503 errors after deploy should follow Debug 503 After Deploy.

concepts.title: Rate Limits
concepts.why: Rate-limit configuration is a known cause of 503-like spikes after deploy.
concepts.summary: Rate Limits controls request throttling in API Gateway through request-per-second, burst, and mode settings.
caveats: Use `shadow` mode before enforcing a large limit change.
A bad rate-limit value can look like a 503 spike when upstream services are healthy.
After changing this config, run Request Routing Tests.

concepts.title: Gateway Operations Manual
concepts.why: Provides source support for gateway deploy and 503 incident handling.
concepts.summary: Elevated 503s after deploy should be diagnosed through gateway logs, route changes, auth failures, and rate-limit config.
caveats: This is a fictional source for demonstration. In a real OKF bundle, this file should link to the live runbook or incident-management documentation.
citations: Fictional gateway operations manual for the OKF demo bundle.

concepts.title: Service Architecture Note
concepts.why: Provides source support for API Gateway responsibilities and constraints.
concepts.summary: API Gateway handles authentication, rate limits, routing, metrics, and logs. Customer routes must pass through auth middleware.
caveats: This is a fictional source for demonstration. In a real OKF bundle, this file should include an ADR, design doc, or reviewed architecture note.
citations: Fictional service architecture note for the OKF demo bundle.
